fundamentals.name: Astera Labs, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Astera Labs, Inc. designs, manufactures, and sells semiconductor-based connectivity solutions for cloud and AI infrastructure. It offers an intelligent connectivity platform, comprised of semiconductor-based, high-speed, mixed-signal connectivity products that integrate a matrix of microcontrollers and sensors, and COSMOS. The company provides COSMOS software suite, a connectivity system management and optimization software that enables management and optimization of resources for large fleets at cloud-scale via link, fleet, and RAS management capabilities. In addition, it offers PCIe/CXL smart DSP retimers and cable modules; ethernet smart cable modules for cloud and AI infrastructure; CXL memory connectivity controllers; and smart fabric switches. The company serves hyperscalers and system original equipment manufacturers. Astera Labs, Inc. was incorporated in 2017 and is based in San Jose, California.

judgment.summary: Astera Labs exhibits exceptional growth in revenue and earnings, coupled with strong margins and a healthy balance sheet, though it trades at a premium valuation.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat consensus by 13.5%; next earnings date is 2026-08-04.
positives: Revenue growth 93.4% YoY and quarterly earnings growth 152.4% indicate robust top-line and bottom-line expansion.
Gross margin of 76.0% and operating margin of 20.1% demonstrate strong profitability.
Return on equity at 21.1% and return on assets at 10.1% show efficient use of capital.
Free cash flow of $240.00M and a high current ratio of 11.30 suggest solid financial health.
Analyst consensus is a strong buy with a mean target price of $244.10.
negatives: Trailing P/E of 208.76 and Price/Sales of 52.53 indicate a very high valuation.
Debt/Equity ratio of 2.80 suggests significant leverage.
Beta of 3.36 implies higher volatility compared to the broader market.
The mean analyst target price of $244.10 is below the current spot price of $306.88.

news.paragraph: Astera Labs (ALAB) has seen significant positive attention this week, with multiple outlets highlighting its strong performance and potential as a top semiconductor stock. Jim Cramer advised holding the stock, and recent reports indicate it is "roaring higher." The company also recently beat its Q1 earnings estimates, with the next earnings date set for August 4th.
